text:
The quality and safety of soy sauce-aroma type baijiu (SSAB) are ultimately determined by the nature of the raw material — the base liquor. Using headspace solid-phase microextraction gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (HS-SPME-GC-MS) and electronic nose (E-nose) technology, this study explored and identified the potential chemical markers at different quality levels of base liquor from 3rd, 4th and 5th generations of liquors.

Correlation of Chemical Composition, Sensory Evaluation and Instrumental Analysis with Quality Grades of Shao Xing Artesanal Baijiu Objectives: To correlate chemical composition, sensory evaluation and E-nose (instrumental) data with various quality grades of Shao Xing Artesanal Baijiu (SSAB). Methods: Seventy-eight batches of SSAB samples at different stages of production were assessed by quantitative descriptive analysis (QDA) and evaluated using an E-nose. Un-targeted metabolomics and correlation analyses were adopted to clarify the key factors affecting the quality of SSAB. Results: The aroma descriptors contributed to discrimination between higher quality grades of SSAB, including sweet and spicy sauce-like odours, floral, fruity and even a little bit of fermented odours, and also better taste characteristics, such as fullness, harmony and persistence. The E-nose data showed that the intensity of the aroma profiles of higher quality grades of SSAB were higher in aromatics, sulfur compounds and alcohols. The 16 un-targeted differential metabolites identified across all rounds of fermentation were 11 esters, 2 aldehydes, 2 ketones and 1 alcohol. Principal component analysis (PCA) showed that differentiated the high and low quality grades of SSAB showed higher abundance of esters and alcohols in the higher quality grades of SSAB, resulting in a more intense fruity smell. Specific compounds like furfuryl ethyl ether, ethyl butanoate and 3-methyl-1-butanol had a significant correlation with the sensor responses of the E-nose. Conclusion: This study provides a digital method for quality grading of SSAB and has provided some useful tips for manufacturers to determine the blending and aging process for production of SSAB.
